element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial rooms teaching directional controls and block manipulation
Collecting scattered hearts to unlock treasure chest and gem
Enemies with predictable patterns; learning to avoid or use shots
Shooting enemies to encapsulate them in eggs; resource management
Using encapsulated enemies as moveable obstacles or bridges
Using eggs as bridges or acquiring bridge-building power-ups
More complex enemy pattern requiring tactical egg placement
Enemies with different physics properties affecting strategy
Non-lethal obstacles requiring navigation or elimination strategy
Temporary abilities that open new puzzle solutions per screen
Balancing limited shots against multiple simultaneous threats
Enemies requiring alternative strategies or avoidance-only tactics

typical_relief_triggers: Collecting a heart to progress toward gem unlock
Successfully encasing an enemy in egg
Opening treasure chest and acquiring gem
Completing room and progressing to next level
Pushing egg to safe storage location
typical_tension_triggers: Limited shots remaining vs. multiple enemies present
Running out of safe spaces to move
Being trapped by egg placement or enemy position
Heart collection incomplete; gem chest still locked
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Puzzle-driven tension with episodic relief. Each room is a self-contained puzzle-action scenario. Tension builds through room-specific constraints (enemy placement, limited shots, complex layouts). Relief comes from solving the puzzle and advancing. Early floors have frequent relief windows; later floors compress multiple tension sources into single rooms with fewer escape options.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Multi-path layouts in later floors; some rooms contain alternative strategies for reaching scattered hearts

difficulty_curve.early_game: Floors 1-3 teach core mechanics individually: movement, block pushing, basic enemy avoidance, shot mechanics. Single threats per room. High safety margins.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Floors 4-6 combine mechanics: multi-enemy rooms, limited shot economy, spatial constraints. Introduction of impeding enemies and immune enemy types. Tension escalates through compressed puzzle design.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Floors 7-10 demand mastery of all mechanics simultaneously. Complex enemy combinations, minimal shot allocation, intricate block configurations. Puzzle solutions require forward planning across multiple moves.
notable_spikes: Floor 3: Introduction of Medusa (ranged enemy) and Rocky (physics-altering enemy) simultaneously
Floor 6-7: Transition from individual mechanic mastery to systems-level puzzle solving with multiple simultaneous constraints

notable_level_patterns.description: Early floors (1-2) arrange enemies and hearts in sequential lines, training players to execute single strategies in isolation
Mid floors (3-5) create rooms where block positioning and enemy egg placement become load-bearing to the solution; limited safe zones
Late floors (7-10) force simultaneous management of enemy types, shot economy, block positioning, and heart collection into single room configurations
Recurring pattern across all floors: limiting shot count to force tactical prioritization of which enemies to encapsulate vs. which to avoid
Mid-game pattern requiring players to use shot eggs as bridges or acquire bridge-building power to cross water barriers
